Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0x15E0 "ROCKETROGER-CC00-15E0" -Di_irq_exit=1 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 jsr $cc00 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Rocket Roger (J1).prg"
Metadata.Notes:
This game has a BASIC program to provide instructions before the main game loads. However, as the main game code overwrites the custom charmap when loaded, the following steps were taken to retain the instructions:
1. The BASIC instructions program was modified at line 60 to remove the tape loading command after exiting the instructions (which used a series of pokes to modify the keyboard buffer), and replaced with a SYS call to jump back to a new machine code routine at $CD00 2. The custom charmap used in the instructions at $2000 was dumped (2048 bytes) 3. A breakpoint at $15E0 was set to break when the main game had loaded 4. The 2048 bytes of data held at $2000 was copied to $D000 5. The custom charmap data used by the instructions is loaded back to $2000 6. A routine was added at $CC00 which sets the correct VICII and BASIC pointer values before executing the BASIC RUN command, which runs the modified instructions program 7. This routine also disables the RUN/STOP key by modifying the STOP vector by setting $E1 at $0328 8. Once the instructions program has finished, the added SYS call jumps to the new routine at $CD00, which restores the 2048 bytes of data from $D000 back to $2000 before jumping to the games entry point at $15E0
